rules: Do not claim success without verification
Prefer editing existing files over rewriting everything
If tests fail, analyze the failure before editing again

safety_policies: avoid destructive commands unless explicitly required

available_tools.name: read_file
available_tools.description: Read the contents of a file

available_tools.name: edit_file
available_tools.description: Edit an existing file by replacing a string pattern

available_tools.name: test_code
available_tools.description: Run the test suite or specific test file

user_input: Fix the transaction isolation level in db/session.py. The default is currently AUTOCOMMIT which causes consistency issues under concurrent load. Change it to READ_COMMITTED.

assistant_trace.goal: Fix transaction isolation level for consistency
assistant_trace.decision: Inspect transaction and session setup before editing
assistant_trace.why: Isolation level may be defined in either the transaction layer or the session factory
hypotheses: Isolation is set in db/session.py session factory
Isolation is set in db/transactions.py transaction decorator

assistant_trace.summary: Loaded session factory configuration — isolation_level is set to AUTOCOMMIT

assistant_trace.why: session.py contains the default isolation level as a string literal — changing it here is the minimal change point
rejected_options: add isolation_level parameter to session factory

assistant_trace.summary: Applied isolation level change from AUTOCOMMIT to READ_COMMITTED
3 tests passed — isolation level change is correct

assistant_trace.content: Updated the default isolation level in db/session.py from AUTOCOMMIT to READ_COMMITTED and verified that all 3 transaction tests pass.

final_response: Updated the default isolation level in db/session.py from AUTOCOMMIT to READ_COMMITTED and verified that all 3 transaction tests pass.

quality.reasoning: Excellent trajectory. Read-before-edit pattern followed, minimal patch applied, verification confirmed with passing test results.
